topics.title: Europe swelters under record heat
topics.summary: A powerful early-summer heatwave is breaking records across Europe, closing schools, straining hospitals and pushing officials to warn people to check on vulnerable neighbours.
topics.discussionPrompt: Heatwaves can really change a normal day. What is your favourite low-tech way to stay cool when the weather gets ridiculous?

topics.title: TSX rebounds with industrials leading
topics.summary: Canada's main stock index rose Thursday, helped by industrial shares and upbeat U.S. economic data that improved investor sentiment after the previous day's commodity-driven slide.
topics.discussionPrompt: Markets seem to react to everything at once. Do you follow these swings closely, or only notice them when they affect everyday prices?

topics.title: Chip stocks jump on AI demand
topics.summary: Micron's strong results and outlook lifted chip stocks worldwide, renewing Wall Street enthusiasm for AI infrastructure and the memory chips needed to power it.
topics.discussionPrompt: AI keeps turning up in market news. Do you think this boom feels practical and real, or a bit like another tech gold rush?

topics.title: Gulf allies seek role in Iran talks
topics.summary: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio told Gulf Arab partners they will be kept involved as Washington pursues talks aimed at ending the war with Iran.
topics.discussionPrompt: Diplomacy often depends on who gets a seat at the table. Is being consulted enough, or do neighbours need real influence in peace talks?
